mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of historical instances where UNRATE crosses the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive rank IC over 60-day forward returns across multiple recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which signals immediate economic momentum and triggers positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with a mean forward return below zero during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated while the federal funds rate stays low, markets price in aggressive future tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days due to expected margin compression.
failure_condition: The combination of high CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS yields positive 20-day returns in >50% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and supports aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns, with a rank IC consistently below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction or growth below threshold levels signals insufficient aggregate demand to sustain corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 falls below the specified threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with stable or falling unemployment indicates efficient capacity utilization without labor market overheating, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE produces negative 5-day returns in >55% of observations.
